These are reasonable given the analysis.\n- **Conclusion:** Valid. These are appropriate.\n- **Conclusion:** Valid.\n\n---\n\n### Summary and Final Justification:\n\n- All reports correctly identify the code as a benign, autogenerated interface for interacting with a known smart contract.\n- No signs of malware, obfuscation, or malicious behavior are present.\n- The scores assigned (malware = 0, risk scores very low) are consistent with the static, utility nature of the code.\n\n### My overall assessment:\n\n- **Malware score:** 0 for all reports — accurate.\n- **Obfuscated score:** 0 for all reports — accurate.\n- **Risk score:** Given the code's static, read-only nature and its purpose as a contract interface, a risk score of around 0.05–0.1 is reasonable.
